dollar_amounts: $87,130.49 (prior cash proffer for Gulick Mill Road)
2026-07-07:4.item_document: Item 04 FY2027-FY2032 Secondary Road Six Year Plan.pdf

2026-07-07:5.summary: A utility substation (Barrister Substation) is proposed on approximately 20-24 acres in Sterling District, requiring special exceptions to permit the utility use in research/development and office park zoning and to reduce parking requirements. The applicant has enhanced screening and buffering along future Thumb Drive to address visual impacts.

2026-07-07:6.summary: Lououn County Board of Supervisors is being asked to approve a special exception to allow Dominion Energy to build the Aspen Substation, a utility substation in the Leesburg district, with modifications to buffer requirements and parking ratios, along with enhanced screening and landscaping measures to address community concerns about visual impacts.

2026-07-07:7.summary: The Board of Supervisors is being asked to authorize the Rural Economic Development Council to develop a white paper exploring agrivoltaics—the combined use of agricultural land for both farming and solar energy production—to identify opportunities that support farming, rural economic development, and clean energy generation in Loudoun County.
2026-07-07:7.item_document: Item 07 BMI-Development of Agrivoltaics White Paper.pdf

2026-07-07:I-2.summary: The county presents a white paper about electrical substations—facilities that adjust and distribute electricity—to inform policy development regarding their siting, screening, and design, particularly as data center demand drives the need for new substations throughout Loudoun County.
2026-07-07:I-2.fiscal_impact: This item has no fiscal impact.
2026-07-07:I-2.item_document: Item I-2 Electrical Substations White Paper.pdf
